project.name: Fuel Resilience AU
project.purpose: Public-interest Australian resilience dashboard that separates verified public data from missing, stale, partial, manual and source-gated feeds.

public_health_signals.label: GitHub Pages deploy
public_health_signals.plain_english: The public site is deployed through the GitHub Pages workflow on pushes to main and can also be run manually.

public_health_signals.label: CI validation
public_health_signals.plain_english: Pull requests and pushes run compiled-dashboard checks, the data validator, the source manifest check, unit tests and browser smoke tests.

public_health_signals.label: Weekly data refresh
public_health_signals.plain_english: Programmatic sources are checked, fetched, validated and committed only after validation passes. Manual source snapshots remain human-reviewed.

public_health_signals.label: Manual data review
public_health_signals.plain_english: A weekly advisory review reports stale, unavailable or manual sources without inventing replacement values.

public_health_signals.label: Source registry
public_health_signals.plain_english: Each dashboard source is expected to carry publisher, cadence, rights, citation, reuse notes, fetch mode and dependent surfaces.

public_health_signals.label: Browser source manifest
public_health_signals.plain_english: Dashboard pages read from a manifest that decides whether generated or manual envelopes should be loaded first.

source_confidence_bands.label: Verified programmatic feeds
source_confidence_bands.render_rule: Show values only when the envelope status is ok and freshness metadata is valid.
source_confidence_bands.meaning: Fetched from a named public machine-readable endpoint and validated before publication.

source_confidence_bands.label: Manual public-source snapshots
source_confidence_bands.render_rule: Label as manual and keep source, period, retrieval timestamp and caveats visible.
source_confidence_bands.meaning: Hand-keyed from a named public document or page where machine retrieval is blocked, unstable or inappropriate.

source_confidence_bands.label: Derived from named envelopes
source_confidence_bands.render_rule: Show derivation context and keep the parent source traceable.
source_confidence_bands.meaning: Calculated or selected from one or more named source envelopes, not invented independently.

source_confidence_bands.label: Partial or stale
source_confidence_bands.render_rule: Show warning language beside the value and do not promote it as complete or current.
source_confidence_bands.meaning: A public source exists, but the coverage, geography, product, timing or cadence is limited.

source_confidence_bands.label: Source-gated or unavailable
source_confidence_bands.render_rule: Render source unavailable, show the missing feed and name the likely holder or next source action where known.
source_confidence_bands.meaning: The dashboard does not have a verified public field, period, unit and reuse-rights path.

trust_rules: Unavailable rows stay visible as public-data gaps rather than being hidden.
Manual public-source snapshots must stay labelled as manual.
A page must not describe itself as live or current unless its loaded envelopes support that claim.
Source freshness, missing data and source-gated fields are evidence, not embarrassment.

known_gaps.gap: Station availability, regional outage feeds, terminal inventory, tanker timing and contract-backed delivery visibility remain source-gated or unavailable.
Land parcels, proponents, bid counts, project delivery status, contracts, capacity and approvals remain source-gated or unavailable.
Fertiliser cover, farm diesel risk, farm-gate fertiliser prices, water allocation, drought pressure, planting pressure and freight disruption still need source-safe feeds.
Sensitive or unsupported operational fields are not asserted without official public evidence.
Housing stress, rental stress, negative-gearing effects and Australian AI workforce exposure remain source-gated until source-safe models or datasets exist.

next_build_steps: Generate this manifest from CI, workflow metadata, source_manifest.json and data/sources.yml instead of maintaining the summary by hand.
Add a compact trust card to the homepage once the page copy is reviewed.
Add a validation script that fails closed if the trust manifest references missing paths or invalid workflow files.
Expose latest workflow run conclusions if a safe public API path is added later.
Extend the manifest with dashboard-level source counts after the source manifest shape is locked.
claim_boundary: This trust layer reports repository health signals, source-governance rules and known data gaps. It does not certify the dashboard, validate every upstream source, or claim official government status.
